name: Roger Miller
abstract: Roger Dean Miller Sr. (January 2, 1936 – October 25, 1992) was an American singer-songwriter, widely known for his honky-tonk-influenced novelty songs and his chart-topping country hits "King of the Road", "Dang Me", and "England Swings".

concerts.label: The Lively Ones, starring Roger Miller and The Good Time Singers

works.t: You Can't Rollerskate in a Buffalo Herd

works_created.t: Big River: Waitin' for the Light to Shine
You Can't Rollerskate in a Buffalo Herd

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Baker
about_quotes.evidence: My parents listened to the usual run-of-the-mill, middle-of-the-road Tijuana Brass, Roger Miller, Al Hirt — you know, the usual things that people had in their record collections in those days.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Baker interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Everybody’s doing Roger Miller, the Four Seasons, and Frankie Vallee. Make some hits.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: The… Roger Miller—the King of the Road, you know. That was the period—the ’60s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
